evidence.text: Company: Meridian Health Systems9
Website:
Industry:
Question: Why are strategic initiatives slowing down even though teams report that work is moving?
Context:
Context:
Meridian is a mid-sized healthcare technology company preparing for enterprise expansion.
Messy Data:
The customer success team says implementation issues are being solved locally, but lessons rarely make it into shared documentation.
Product says it often learns about customer workflow problems through informal Slack threads instead of a consistent feedback process.
Several teams mentioned that decisions wait for executive approval, even when the work is operational and low-risk.
A program manager said no one is sure who owns cross-functional follow-up after launch meetings.
Engineering noted that similar integration issues keep repeating across customer accounts.
Operations said teams are moving quickly inside their own functions, but handoffs between functions are inconsistent.
A VP said centralized review helps avoid bad decisions, but it also slows execution when demand spikes.
Sales said customer commitments sometimes get made before implementation capacity is confirmed.
Customer success said support knowledge stays with individual account managers.
Leadership believes the organization is scaling, but several teams described the same coordination and knowledge-retention problems recurring.
